summary: Hooks run on every tool use - keep them under 200ms or you will feel the lag on every single action
problem: You add a hook script that does useful work but takes 500ms-1s to run, and every file read, edit, and bash call in the session now has that overhead
solution: Profile your hooks with `time` before deploying them. Move anything slow (network calls, large file reads, heavy computation) to async background jobs that write to an inbox file the hook reads instead
root_cause: Hooks are synchronous gate checks on the hot path of every tool call. There is no batching or caching; 500ms per hook becomes seconds of dead time per complex task
